Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: A pitot-static smart probe delivers these three core flight parameters to the air data system.
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: From total and static pressures, the smart probe supplies airspeed, altitude, and vertical speed. OAT comes from a separate probe, engine data from engine instruments, and autopilot commands are AFCS outputs.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: This defining feature sets a 'smart' pitot-static probe apart from a conventional pneumatic one.
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: The integrated digital processor converts pressure directly into digital air data, which is what makes it 'smart.' Body material, manual calibration, and a heater alone do not define the smart probe concept.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: This installation advantage is the main reason manufacturers are replacing traditional pitot-static plumbing with smart probes.
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: Smart probes replace long pneumatic tubing runs with short electrical wiring, reducing installation complexity. Maintenance is similar or reduced, cockpit displays are often unchanged, and lifecycle cost varies by program.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: This is the pressure that the static port on a pitot-static probe samples from the airstream.
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: The static port senses ambient atmospheric pressure independent of ram effect. Ram pressure is sensed by the pitot inlet, cabin pressure is measured separately, and EGT is a temperature, not a pressure.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: Field data shows this failure mode is the dominant cause of pitot-static smart probe discrepancies.
choices: Blockage of ports by ice or debris
Heater element open from thermal stress
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: Port blockage by ice, insects, or debris dominates smart probe discrepancy data. Firmware, heater, and output stage failures are less common and well detected by BIT.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: The heating element inside a pitot probe draws heavy current for this specific reason.
choices: To warm air for temperature sensing
To prevent ice from blocking the ports
To supply power to the processor chip
To stabilize the sensor temperature only
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: Pitot heat prevents ice from forming over the ports at high altitude and in visible moisture. Sensor temperature is controlled by separate means, the processor has its own supply, and OAT sensing requires sampled air to be unheated.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: This digital protocol is typically used by smart probes to transmit air data to the avionics suite.
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: ARINC 429 is the standard digital air data bus. Analog voltage is obsolete for air data, RF telemetry is inappropriate inside the airframe, and mechanical cables are not used.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: Among all possible causes, this is the single most common source of inaccurate pitot-static smart probe readings.
choices: A malfunction in the air data computer
A loss of GPS receiver signal lock
Blockage of the pressure sensing ports
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: Port blockage from ice, insects, or debris is overwhelmingly the most common cause of inaccurate pitot-static readings. ADC faults, GPS loss, and radar interference are comparatively rare and have different symptoms.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: Outside of deep-inspection intervals, this is the chief routine maintenance action performed on a pitot-static smart probe.
choices: Visual inspection of ports for contamination
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: Routine visual inspection of the ports for contamination is the primary between-check task. Sensor recalibration is rare, heaters are not scheduled for replacement, and firmware updates occur on bulletin.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: In a pitot-static smart probe, this is the specific pressure the pitot tube element senses.
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: The pitot tube senses total pressure (ambient plus ram). Static ports sense ambient only, vertical speed is a rate of change, and there is no temperature-compensated cabin pressure in this context.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: When a smart probe shows inconsistent preflight data, these two items should be inspected first.
choices: The wiring connections and the port condition
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: Wiring issues and port contamination are the most common causes of inconsistent data. Firmware version, calibration date, and power supply regulation are secondary checks reserved for deeper troubleshooting.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: The microprocessor inside a smart probe offers this key capability to support maintenance actions.
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: Built-in test continuously monitors probe health and reports faults. Weight, mechanical durability, and multi-type sensing are not processor-driven benefits.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: Should a smart pitot-static probe malfunction during cruise, this is the principal hazard it creates for the flight deck.
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: A failed probe feeds wrong airspeed and altitude to pilots and autopilot, a well-known loss-of-control precursor. Comms, display blanking, and fuel burn are not direct consequences.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: An ADC derives airspeed and altitude by processing this pair of incoming pressure signals.
choices: Pitot total and static ambient pressure
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: The ADC derives airspeed and altitude from pitot total pressure and static ambient pressure. GPS values are separate position/speed data, manifold pressure is an engine parameter, and fuel quantity is unrelated.

Smart Probes & Air Data.clue: In an aircraft fitted with several pitot-static probes, this situation activates the 'unreliable airspeed' alert for the pilots.
choices: Loss of GPS satellite signal lock
Partial blockage on a single probe only
A manual disconnect of the autopilot
Smart Probes & Air Data.explanation: Multi-probe systems compare independent sources and annunciate unreliable airspeed when they disagree beyond threshold. A single partial blockage may not exceed threshold, and GPS and autopilot events are unrelated to airspeed validity.
